sentences.text: Acupuncture and Dry Needling Number: 0135 Applicable CPT / HCPCS / ICD-10 Codes Scope of Policy This Clinical Policy Bulletin addresses acupuncture and dry needling.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers acupuncture (manual or electroacupuncture) medically necessary for any of the following indications: A. Chronic (minimum 12 weeks duration) neck pain; or B. Chronic (minimum 12 weeks duration) headache; or C. Low back pain; or D. Nausea of pregnancy; or E. Pain from osteoarthritis of the knee or hip (adjunctive therapy); or F. Post-operative and chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting; or G. Post-operative dental pain; or H. Temporomandibular disorders (TMD).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Maintenance treatment, where the member's symptoms are neither regressing or improving, is considered not medically necessary.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: If no clinical benefit is appreciated after four weeks of acupuncture, then the treatment plan should be reevaluated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Further acupuncture treatment is not considered medically necessary if the member does not demonstrate meaningful improvement in symptoms. II.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven The following procedures are considered experimental, investigational, or unproven because the effectiveness of these approaches has not been established: A. sentences.text: Policy Limitations and Exclusions Note: Standard Aetna plans extend coverage of acupuncture for medically necessary indications when administered by a health care provider practicing within the scope of his/her license.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Some Aetna plans limit coverage of acupuncture to when it is used in a lieu of other anesthesia for a surgical or dental procedure covered under the health benefits plan, and the health care provider administering it is a legally qualified physician practicing within the scope of his/her license.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Some other plans may extend coverage of acupuncture for medically necessary indications, but only when administered by a health care provider who is a legally qualified physician practicing within the scope of his/her license.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Please check benefit plan descriptions for details.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The general theory of acupuncture is based on the premise that there are patterns of energy flow (Qi) through the body that are essential for health.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Disruptions of this flow are believed to be responsible for disease.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Acupuncture may correct imbalances of flow at identifiable points close to the skin.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Findings from basic research have begun to elucidate the mechanisms of action of acupuncture, including the release of opioids and other peptides in the central nervous system and the periphery and changes in neuroendocrine function.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: While there have been many studies of its potential usefulness, the vast majority of papers studying acupuncture in the biomedical literature consist of case reports, case series, or intervention studies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: One of the difficulties with drawing conclusions from the existing literature is that the term acupuncture is used to describe a variety of treatments that differ in many important aspects according to level of effect (e.g., local, segmental, generalized) and type of acupuncture treatment (e.g., manual versus electrical acupuncture).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Many of these studies provide equivocal results because of design, sample size, and other factors.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The issue is further complicated by inherent difficulties in the use of appropriate controls, such as placebos and sham acupuncture groups, and by absence of studies comparing acupuncture with conventional biomedical treatments.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Some factors needing investigation include frequency, number, and duration of treatments, depth of puncture, number of acupuncture points used, combination with other therapies, sample size, setting, blinding factors, and needle size.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Be that as it may, promising results have emerged on the efficacy of acupuncture in adult post-operative and chemotherapy nausea and vomiting and in postoperative dental pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There is insufficient evidence of the efficacy of acupuncture as a treatment for fibromyalgia.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Public Health Service, Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ) performed a technology assessment (2003) on Acupuncture for the Treatment of Fibromyalgia; it stated that "[a]t this time, therefore, there is insufficient evidence to conclude that acupuncture has efficacy for the treatment of fibromyalgia."
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There is evidence to support the use of acupuncture in migraine.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a large randomized controlled study (n = 401), Vickers et al (2004) examined the effects of a policy of "use acupuncture" on headache (predominantly migraine), health status, days off sick, and use of resources in patients with chronic headache compared with a policy of "avoid acupuncture".
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients were randomly allocated to receive up to 12 acupuncture treatments over 3 months or to a control intervention offering usual care.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Headache score, SF-36 health status, and use of medication were assessed at baseline, 3, and 12 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Use of resources was assessed every 3 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Headache score at 12 months, the primary end point, was lower in the acupuncture group (16.2, SD 13.7, n = 161, 34 % reduction from baseline) than in controls (22.3, SD 17.0, n = 140, 16 % reduction from baseline).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The adjusted difference between means is 4.6 (95 % confidence interval [CI]: 2.2 to 7.0; p = 0.0002).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This result is robust to sensitivity analysis incorporating imputation for missing data.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients in the acupuncture group experienced the equivalent of 22 fewer days of headache per year (8 to 38).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: SF-36 data favored acupuncture, although differences reached significance only for physical role functioning, energy, and change in health.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Compared with controls, patients randomized to acupuncture used 15 % less medication (p = 0.02), made 25 % fewer visits to general practitioners (p = 0.10), and took 15 % fewer days off sick (p = 0.2).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that acupuncture leads to persisting, clinically relevant benefits for primary care patients with chronic headache, particularly migraine.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The results of the study by Vickers et al (2004) are in agreement with recent findings of Allais et al (2003) who reported that acupuncture is effective in reducing the frequency of migraine attacks as well as those by Linde et al (2009) who reported that acupuncture was more effective than a placebo injection in the early treatment of an acute migraine attack.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Facco and colleagues (2008) examined the effectiveness of a true acupuncture treatment according to traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) in migraine without aura, comparing it to a standard mock acupuncture protocol, an accurate mock acupuncture healing ritual, and untreated controls.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A prospective, randomized, controlled study was performed in 160 patients suffering from migraine without aura, assessed according to the ICD-10 classification.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients were divided into the following 4 groups: I. group TA, treated with true acupuncture (according to TCM) plus rizatriptan; II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: group RMA, treated with ritualized mock acupuncture plus rizatriptan; III.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: group SMA, treated with standard mock acupuncture plus rizatriptan; and IV.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: group R, without prophylactic treatment with relief therapy only (rizatriptan).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The MIDAS Questionnaire was administered before treatment (T0), at 3 (T1) and 6 months (T2) from the beginning of treatment, and the MIDAS Index (MI) was calculated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Rizatriptan intake was also checked in all groups of patients at T0, T1, and T2.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Group TA and RMA were evaluated according to TCM as well; then, the former was submitted to true acupuncture and the latter to mock acupuncture treatment resembling the same as TA.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The statistical analysis was conducted with factorial ANOVA and multiple tests with a Bonferroni adjustment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 127 patients completed the study (33 dropouts): 32 belonged to group TA, 30 to group RMA, 31 to group SMA, and 34 to group R. Before treatment the MI (T(0)) was moderate-to- severe with no significant inter-group differences.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: All groups underwent a decrease of MI at T(1) and T(2), with a significant group difference at both T(1) and T(2) compared to T(0) (p L) and improved speech and swallowing.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, she did not continue to receive the Enercel acupoint injections, and she subsequently demonstrated a slow, progressive loss of neurological function during the ensuing 3 months, as shown on follow-up examinations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patient 2 had significantly impaired speech and mild motor loss in the upper extremities and the left leg.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: After 4 weeks of treatment, his voice had significantly improved to the point where his speech was understandable and his motor functions had returned to normal.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: He continued receiving Enercel acupoint injections during the 3-month follow-up period and his clinical improvements were maintained.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Thus, these 2 patients with ALS showed clinical improvements after 4 weeks of Enercel acupoint injection therapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Follow-up data suggested that ongoing therapy may be necessary in order to maintain these positive effects.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this preliminary study merits further study and confirmation.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a Cochrane review, Paley et al (2011) evaluated effectiveness of acupuncture for relief of cancer-related pain in adults.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CENTRAL, MEDLINE, EMBASE, PsycINFO, AMED, and SPORTDiscus were searched up to November 2010 including non-English language papers.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Randomized controlled trials evaluating any type of invasive acupuncture for pain directly related to cancer in adults of 18 years or over were selected for analysis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: It was planned to pool data to provide an overall measure of effect and to calculate the number needed to treat to benefit, but this was not possible due to heterogeneity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Two review authors independently extracted data adding it to data extraction sheets. Quality scores were given to studies.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Data sheets were compared and discussed with a third review author who acted as arbiter.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A total of 3 RCTs (204 participants) were included.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: One high-quality study investigated the effect of auricular acupuncture compared with auricular acupuncture at "placebo" points and with non-invasive vaccaria ear seeds attached at "placebo" points.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participants in 2 acupuncture groups were blinded but blinding wasn't possible in the ear seeds group because seeds were attached using tape.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This may have biased results in favor of acupuncture groups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participants in the real acupuncture group had lower pain scores at 2-month follow-up than either the placebo or ear seeds group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There was high-risk of bias in 2 studies because of low methodological quality.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: One study comparing acupuncture with medication concluded that both methods were effective in controlling pain, although acupuncture was the most effective.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The second study compared acupuncture, point-injection and medication in participants with stomach cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Long-term pain relief was reported for both acupuncture and point-injection compared with medication during the last 10 days of treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although both studies have positive results in favor of acupuncture, they should be viewed with caution due to methodological limitations, small sample sizes, poor reporting and inadequate analysis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that there is insufficient evidence to judge whether acupuncture is effective in treating cancer- related pain in adults.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a pilot study, park et al (2011) examined the possibility of Carthami-Semen (CS, Safflower seed) acupuncture point injection as a new promising treatment for chronic daily headache (CDH).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 40 subjects with CDH were recruited and randomized to a CS acupuncture point injection group or a normal saline (NS) acupuncture point injection group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Acupuncture point injections were applied twice-weekly during a 4-week period to the bilateral Fengchi (GB20), Jianjing (GB21) and Taiyang (EX-HN5) acupoints with CS extract or NS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The primary outcome measure was headache-related QOL (HR-QOL), assessed using the Headache Impact Test (HIT).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Secondary outcome measures were the changes in the number of headache-free days and health status as assessed with the Short Form (36) Health Survey (SF-36).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: HIT scores decreased by 14.9 points in the CS acupuncture point injection group compared with 7.9 points in the NS acupuncture point injection group (p = 0.013).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Headache-free days increased by 32.6 % in the CS acupuncture point injection group compared with 17.4 % in the NS acupuncture point injection group (p = 0.045).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There were significant increases in SF-36 scores compared with baseline in both groups, but the mean improvement was greater in the CS acupuncture point injection group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No serious adverse events were reported.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that these findings suggested that the CS acupuncture point injection could be a new safe and promising treatment for CDH.
